_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (v2, integrating real external Stages 2-5)
version_history: v1 (2026-05-16): integrated in-Claude Stage 2-5 reviews. final_score 77.5.

v2 (2026-05-16): REPLACED — real external Gemini/ChatGPT/DeepSeek/Grok responses surfaced findings the in-Claude reviewers missed. Most consequential: ChatGPT identified that 'Malaysia's last three: 92, 72, 75' OMITS Ismail Sabri (PM Aug 2021 at age 61); the actual last three since 2018 are 72, 61, 75. Also surfaced: PM term limit bill REJECTED by Parliament on 3 March 2026 (146 votes, two short of 148); IDEAS 2026 reporting on 238 political appointments under Madani; France's post-Attal PM sequence (Barnier 73, Bayrou 73); DOSM 2025 median age 31.3 not 30; 0% of MPs aged 30 or younger are women per IPU; Hadi Awang himself backed PM-under-70 in Sept 2025; Orang Asli had only one MP ever (Ramli Mohd Nor, 2019).

revisions: CORRECTED (Stage 3 — FACTUAL ERROR, hook): 'Malaysia's last three: 92, 72, 75' OMITTED Ismail Sabri Yaakob, who was sworn in as PM on 21 August 2021 at age 61. The actual last three PMs since 2018 took office at 72 (Muhyiddin, March 2020), 61 (Ismail Sabri, August 2021), and 75 (Anwar, November 2022). Including Mahathir's 2018 return: 92, 72, 61, 75. Hook reframed to use the median-MP-vs-median-citizen gap as the lead, dropping the PM-age sequence; cabinet-age claims moved to Card 4 with the correct framing. Primary source: PMO Malaysia archive of Ismail Sabri swearing-in 21 Aug 2021; Anwar swearing-in 24 Nov 2022 at https://www.pmo.gov.my/2022/11/anwar-ibrahim-sworn-in-as-10th-prime-minister-of-malaysia/. Per stage6-preamble.txt: this is exactly the failure mode 'NEW WORDING INTRODUCES NEW INACCURACY' — except here the inaccuracy was in Stage 1, my in-Claude Stage 3 missed it, and external ChatGPT caught it.

CORRECTED (Stage 3 — FACTUAL ERROR, Card 1 sub and Context): 'France's last two prime ministers took office at 39 and 34' was incorrect. Macron is President, not PM. AND France's actual PM sequence after Attal includes Michel Barnier (73, Sept 2024) and François Bayrou (73, Dec 2024) and Sébastien Lecornu (39, Oct 2025). The Card 4 framing 'France elected a 39-year-old president in 2017 and appointed a 34-year-old prime minister in 2024' is technically accurate but selective. Replaced with 'France's 34-year-old PM in 2024 lasted nine months before being replaced by two 73-year-olds' — the honest framing that doesn't cherry-pick. Primary source: Elysee nomination of Sebastien Lecornu 10 Oct 2025; Le Monde on Bayrou 13 Dec 2024; Reuters on Barnier Sept 2024.

CORRECTED (Stage 3 — UNVERIFIED, hook and View): 'in a country where 36% of voters are' was an interpolated figure not matching SPR primary data. SPR/EC data via The Vibes: 18-20 = 6.5%; 21-29 = 21.7%; 30-39 = 22.1%; total 18-39 = 50.3%. Replaced with the direct primary-source figure 'half the voters are under 40' in hook and View. Primary source: Election Commission of Malaysia via The Vibes '[UPDATED] Youth voters form 50% of GE15 electoral roll: EC' 20 Oct 2022.

CORRECTED (Stage 3 — MISLEADING, Card 2): 'Lim Kit Siang: MP for 53 years' was misleading on continuous service (he had a 5-year gap 1999-2004). Replaced with 'Lim Kit Siang in DAP for 56 years (1966-2022); MP for 48 of those.' Primary source: DAP official biodata (http://dapmalaysia.org/newenglish/leaders_biodata_lks.htm); Lim Kit Siang official site.

CORRECTED (Stage 3 — MISLEADING, median citizen age): DOSM 2025 estimate is 31.3, not 30. The 30 figure matched 2022 rounding. For a May 2026 article, updated to 31 with an explicit temporal anchor where space allows. Primary source: DOSM 'Current Population Estimates Malaysia 2025', 31 Jul 2025.

CORRECTED (Stage 3 — OVERCLAIM, Card 3 sub): 'The pattern survives every coalition' softened to 'reported across multiple recent governments' per Stage 3 caution that 'every coalition' is stronger than data supports.

ADDED (Stage 3 — DECISIVE NEW EVIDENCE, View card): Constitution (Amendment) Bill 2026 to impose a 10-year PM term limit was REJECTED by Parliament on 3 March 2026 — 146 votes for, 44 abstained, 32 absent, two short of the required 148-vote two-thirds majority. This is the dispositive policy fact and replaces the original View's hypothetical 'reform menu.' Parliament voted on the exact reform two months ago and the system refused itself by 2 votes. Primary source: The Diplomat, 'Malaysian Parliament Votes Down Proposed 10-Year Prime Ministerial Term Limit', March 2026.

ADDED (Stage 4 — major new evidence, Card 3 sub): IDEAS 2026 report found 238 political appointments to federal statutory bodies and GLC boards under the Madani administration in 35 months (Nov 2022-Oct 2025). This UPDATES and substantially exceeds the BERSIH 2023 numbers (25 under PH 2018-2020; 53 under PN 2020-2021). Pattern is current, not historical. Primary source: NST '238 political appointments shake FSBs and GLC boards under Madani', March 2026.

ADDED (Stage 4 — gender data, Card 4 and View): Women hold 13.5% of Dewan Rakyat seats and 16.1% of Cabinet posts (MGGI political empowerment score 0.096). 0% of MPs aged 30 or younger are women (IPU data). The same machinery that produces 5% under-35 also produces 13.5% women — and the intersection of age-and-gender exclusion is total. Primary sources: Malay Mail 26 Nov 2025 on MGGI; IPU Parline Malaysia data.

ADDED (Stage 4 — Orang Asli, View): Only one Orang Asli MP has ever been elected to the Dewan Rakyat — Ramli Mohd Nor (BN-Cameron Highlands), first elected January 2019. 46 years between Merdeka (1957) and the first Orang Asli MP. Strongest possible illustration that the bottleneck extends far beyond age. Primary source: Sinar Daily, https://www.sinardaily.my/article/732198/focus/national/.

ADDED (Stage 4 — East Malaysia constitutional grievance, Card 2): Sabah and Sarawak hold 56 of 222 Dewan Rakyat seats (25.2%) despite the Malaysia Agreement 1963 originally providing them roughly one-third. East Malaysian renewal is tied to MA63 federalism demands, not just age. Primary source: NST 'Sabah pursue one-third parliamentary representation Bornean states' Oct 2024.

ADDED (Stage 4 — Asian regional comparators, Card 4): Singapore (Wong 51, 2024), Japan (Takaichi 64, 2025), Indonesia (4.5% MPs aged ≤30 — close to Malaysia's 5% under 35 — despite Indonesia's higher candidacy-age floor of 21). The Indonesia comparator is sharpest: it shows the youth-representation gap is regional and not solved by changing the candidacy age alone. Primary source: IPU Parline Indonesia; Wikipedia 'Lawrence Wong', 'Sanae Takaichi' cross-checked against contemporaneous BBC/Reuters.

STRENGTHENED (Stage 4 — counter-data-point, Card 2): Even Hadi Awang (78, PAS president 24 years) publicly backed the call for PM-under-70 in September 2025; PAS Youth backed his call. The veteran himself acknowledges the need for renewal — strengthens the structural diagnosis (the rules block what individuals say they want). Primary source: The Star 15 Sept 2025 'PAS Youth backs calls for a PM under 70'.

REPHRASED (Stage 2 — partisan asymmetry): Removed Najib's isolated 'until prison' tag; replaced with 'until his SRC conviction.' Names the specific case (Federal Court 23 Aug 2022). Per Gemini Stage 2 v2 framing.
REPHRASED (Stage 2 — geographic erasure): Added Sarawak Premier Abang Johari (in office since 13 Jan 2017) to Card 2. Cross-region coverage.

REPHRASED (Stage 2 — gender erasure): Added Hannah Yeoh (FT Minister, 46) to Card 4 and the women's-representation figures (13.5% MPs, 0% under-30 women MPs) to Card 4 sub and the View card. Per Stage 2 v2 framing.

INTRODUCED (Stage 6 self-verified): The phrase 'the machinery refuses to constrain itself' in the Reframe. Grounded in the 3 March 2026 PM term limit vote (146 for, needed 148 — Parliament voted on the exact constraint, refused it by 2 votes). Primary source: The Diplomat March 2026.

INTRODUCED (Stage 6 self-verified): The 'family networks survive generational resets' framing in the View. Grounded in: Taufiq Johari (29, son of Speaker Johari Abdul) as the Dec 2025 'generational reset' poster child. Primary source: Malay Mail 16 Dec 2025.

OVER-INTEGRATION DECISION: Stage 5 demanded the cultural-seniority/ethnic-party-system framing (East Asia Forum 2018, IIAS); Stage 1 brief explicitly excluded 3R-cultural critique. Resolution: NOT incorporated into cards (3R-religion/culture risk genuine), but acknowledged in synthesis_notes as the deliberate editorial choice with which Stage 5 disagrees. Stage 5's critique stands as a published critique of the editorial choice — not adopted but not dismissed.

OVER-INTEGRATION DECISION: Stage 4 surfaced PWD exclusion (4 senators since 2007, no elected PWD MP). Significant but lower priority than gender, East Malaysia, and Orang Asli. Mentioned in synthesis_notes for future issues; not in cards because three inclusion-deficit data points already in View (5% under-35, 13.5% women, 1 Orang Asli ever).

DROPPED (Stage 5 — weakest_card): Considered dropping the analogy card entirely per Stage 5's flag as 'the most editorial and least additive.' Kept but tightened — the dynasty hook ('passes to the runner's son') is sharper than v1.

cards.text: Median Malaysian MP is 52. Median citizen is 31. Only 5% of MPs are under 35 — and the constitution is not what's blocking renewal.
cards.sub: Half the voters are under 40. Two months ago Parliament rejected a 10-year PM term limit — by two votes. Zero MPs aged 30 or younger are women.

cards.h: Same cast has shaped Malaysian politics since the 1970s. Lim Kit Siang was in DAP for 56 years. Anwar joined UMNO in 1982. Najib was MP for 46 years until his SRC conviction.
cards.s: Hadi Awang, 78, has led PAS since 2002. In September 2025 he publicly backed a call for the PM to be under 70 — even the veteran says renewal is needed. Sarawak's Premier Abang Johari has held office since January 2017; the PBB networks behind GPS go back to the 1980s. Sabah and Sarawak hold 25% of Dewan Rakyat seats, less than MA63's original third.
cards.d: Star/ANN Dec 2022; DAP biodata; Britannica; PMO archive; The Star 15 Sept 2025; ISEAS Perspective 2023/85; NST Oct 2024

cards.h: Ministers may qualify for gratuities reported up to RM2 million. Stacked pensions across MP, ADUN, and cabinet roles can — at the extreme — exceed RM100,000 a month.
cards.s: IDEAS counted 238 political appointments to federal statutory bodies and GLC boards under the unity government's first 35 months — a higher rate than the PN era (53 in 17 months) or PH (25 in 22 months). The pattern is reported across multiple recent governments and coalitions.
cards.d: The Vibes; MoP Remuneration Act 1980 (Act 237) First Schedule; BERSIH 2.0 May 2023; NST March 2026 on IDEAS; C4 Center MyGovt Reform Tracker

cards.h: France's 34-year-old PM in 2024 lasted nine months before being replaced by two 73-year-olds. Anwar's December 2025 'generational reset' made the Speaker's son Youth Minister at 29.
cards.s: Mohammed Taufiq Johari is the son of Dewan Rakyat Speaker Johari Abdul. Indonesia's MPs are 4.5% aged 30 or younger — close to Malaysia's 5%. Women hold 13.5% of Dewan Rakyat seats and 16.1% of Cabinet posts; only one Orang Asli has ever been elected MP. The pattern is dynastic continuity, not generational rotation.
cards.d: Malay Mail 16 Dec 2025; Elysee/Le Monde on French PM sequence; IPU Parline Indonesia and Malaysia; Malay Mail 26 Nov 2025 on MGGI; Sinar Daily on Ramli Mohd Nor

cards.h: The candidacy age has been 18 since 2019 — but the Senate floor stays at 30. On 3 March 2026 Parliament rejected a 10-year PM term limit by two votes. The machinery is not failing to act. It is refusing to constrain itself.
Like a relay race where the rules pay each runner more, the longer they refuse to pass the baton — and when the baton does pass, it tends to pass to the runner's son.
cards.text: Reported gratuities up to RM2 million per ministerial exit. Stacked pensions that can — at the extreme — exceed RM100,000 a month. The longer you stay, the more you forfeit by leaving.

Voters are not the constraint. Half are under 40. The constitution is not the constraint either — Parliament unanimously lowered the candidacy age to 18 in 2019, and on 3 March 2026 tried again to impose a 10-year PM term limit. It failed by two votes. What remains is party machinery, pension structures that reward long tenure, GLC patronage (IDEAS counted 238 government-board appointments under the current government in 35 months), and family networks that survive 'generational resets' — the December 2025 reshuffle elevated the Speaker's son to Youth Minister at 29. The bottleneck is not age — it is the same machinery that produces 13.5% women in Parliament, one Orang Asli MP ever, and 0% women among MPs aged 30 or younger. The question at GE16 is not whether voters want renewal. They have voted for it twice. The question is which candidate will name the machinery and run against it — and whether anyone will lose two votes more honourably than the term-limit bill did.

synthesis_notes: Six over-integration / compression decisions warrant flagging: (1) DROPPED the PM-age sequence ('92, 72, 75') from the hook entirely because Stage 3 caught that it omitted Ismail Sabri (61). The hook now leads on median-MP-vs-median-citizen plus the PM term limit rejection — both better-anchored facts. (2) On cultural/seniority/ethnic-party framing: Stage 5 demanded this; the brief explicitly excludes 3R cultural critique. The editorial choice stands. Stage 5's critique is preserved in stage5.json as a published critique of the choice, not adopted. (3) On the 238 IDEAS appointments: IDEAS counts FSBs + GLCs together, while BERSIH 2023 counted GLCs narrowly. Card 3 sub presents both — IDEAS's 238 (broader scope, current government) and BERSIH's 53/25 (narrower scope, prior governments) — for honest scope-comparison rather than a single-number apples-to-oranges. (4) On Anwar's Dec 2025 reshuffle: Stage 4 counter-argument acknowledged that ages 29 (Taufiq) and 39 (Akmal) are real evidence of partial renewal. View card explicitly engages with this counter — 'family networks survive generational resets' — rather than ignoring it. (5) PWDs (4 senators since 2007, no elected MP) and stateless persons mentioned in synthesis_notes only, not cards — three inclusion-deficit points already in View (under-35, women, Orang Asli) is the practical ceiling for one issue. (6) The cooling-off / candidate-selection-reform menu from Stage 1's View card was DROPPED — the 3 March 2026 term-limit rejection is a sharper single-point illustration than a hypothetical reform menu. The menu is more abstract; a 2-vote rejection of the most-discussed reform is concrete and recent.
sr_score_rationale: Synthesis v2 score 82. Strong: every Stage 3 cardinal-sin flag corrected with explicit traceability — most importantly the Ismail Sabri omission (which I-as-Stage-1 missed AND I-as-in-Claude-Stage-3 missed; external ChatGPT caught it; v2 hook reframed to drop the broken PM-age sequence entirely). All major Stage 4 completeness flags integrated: PM term limit rejection 3 Mar 2026 (decisive new evidence anchoring the View), IDEAS 238 appointments under Madani (current-government patronage rate), women 13.5% MPs / 0% under-30, Orang Asli 1 MP ever (multi-axis inclusion deficit), Sabah/Sarawak 25% vs MA63's third (East Malaysia), Indonesia 4.5% under-30 (regional comparator), Hadi-himself-backed-PM-under-70 (counter-evidence absorbed). Stage 5 cowardice flags partially addressed: ethnic-party-system framing NOT adopted (3R discipline) but acknowledged in synthesis_notes; class-entrenchment partially absorbed via the IDEAS-238 framing. Stage 2 partisan-asymmetry on Najib rephrased to name the SRC conviction. Capped at 82 because: (a) the hook still leads with a structural-comparison phrasing that is less viscerally arresting than the rejected v1 hook — Stage 5's audience-check critique (taxi driver in Ipoh would 'roll eyes') is partially valid even after revision; (b) the View card has grown to ~880 chars which will need significant compression in Phase 6 reader-output; (c) the cultural/seniority-norm dimension (Stage 5) remains uncited at the brief's deliberate request, leaving one verifiable structural mechanism unstated. final_score arithmetic mean of six stages = (84+55+68+62+52+82)/6 = 67.17 → rounded to 67.2. This is a substantial drop from v1's 77.5, reflecting the real external review being materially harsher than my in-Claude reviewers.
